heading: Cyberpiracy protections for individuals
label: 8131 Cyberpiracy protections for individuals

children.heading: Civil liability
children.label: A Civil liability
children.text: Any person who registers a domain name that consists of the name of another living person, or a name substantially and confusingly similar thereto, without that person’s consent, with the specific intent to profit from such name by selling the domain name for financial gain to that person or any third party, shall be liable in a civil action by such person.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: A person who in good faith registers a domain name consisting of the name of another living person, or a name substantially and confusingly similar thereto, shall not be liable under this paragraph if such name is used in, affiliated with, or related to a work of authorship protected under title 17, including a work made for hire as defined in , and if the person registering the domain name is the copyright owner or licensee of the work, the person intends to sell the domain name in conjunction with the lawful exploitation of the work, and such registration is not prohibited by a contract between the registrant and the named person. The exception under this subparagraph shall apply only to a civil action brought under paragraph (1) and shall in no manner limit the protections afforded under the Trademark Act of 1946 ( et seq.) or other provision of Federal or State law.section 101 of title 1715 U.S.C. 1051

children.heading: Remedies
children.label: 2 Remedies
children.text: In any civil action brought under paragraph (1), a court may award injunctive relief, including the forfeiture or cancellation of the domain name or the transfer of the domain name to the plaintiff. The court may also, in its discretion, award costs and attorneys fees to the prevailing party.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 3 Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “domain name” has the meaning given that term in section 45 of the Trademark Act of 1946 ().15 U.S.C. 1127

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 4 Effective date
children.text: This section shall apply to domain names registered on or after .November 29, 1999
